title: Match isolation level to the consistency need
description: Pick read-committed, repeatable-read, or serializable deliberately and handle the failure modes each allows.
body: Use when a transaction's correctness depends on what concurrent transactions can do. Do not assume the default read-committed prevents lost updates or read-modify-write races. Actions: for a read-modify-write, use SELECT ... FOR UPDATE or raise to repeatable-read/serializable; understand serializable can abort with a serialization failure that the app must retry. Validation signals include a concurrency test that two transactions racing on the same row produce a correct result (one retries) rather than a lost update. Risk boundary: a balance/inventory update under read-committed without row locking can silently lose writes and is a blocking correctness finding.
